revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川島町]]にある[[円墳]]である。東大塚古墳群を構成する1基。 直径36...'
revision.text:
'''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川島町]]にある[[円墳]]である。東大塚古墳群を構成する1基。
直径36メートル・高さ5.5メートルの円墳と推定されるが、度重なる土取により墳丘は元の形がよくわからないほど大破している。江戸時代文化年間(1807～1817)に墳丘が削平された際に、緑泥片岩製の箱式石棺が掘り出された。この石棺は発見後長らく墳頂に露出放置されていたが、昭和36年1月25日付けで町指定有形文化財に指定された後、川島中学校校門前に移築された。石棺の大きさは、外法長さ2.32メートル・幅0.8メートル、内法長さ1.93メートル・幅0.52メートルである。
副葬品は、石棺内から鉄剣、大刀、鉄鏃、銅製の器が見つかったと伝えられているが、現在それらの遺物は所在不明である。墳丘から出土した埴輪から、6世紀中頃の築造と推定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』pp365-366、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

{{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつかこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]]

{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつかこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:川島町の歴史]]

[[ファイル:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|大塚古墳（2013年9月）]] '''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川島町]]にある[[円墳]]である。東大塚古墳群を構成する1基。
直径36メートル・高さ5.5メートルの円墳と推定されるが、度重なる土取により墳丘は元の形がよくわからないほど大破している。江戸時代文化年間(1807 - 1817)に墳丘が削平された際に、[[緑泥石|緑泥]]片岩製の箱式[[石棺]]が掘り出された。この石棺は発見後長らく墳頂に露出放置されていたが、[[1961年]](昭和36年)1月25日付けで町指定有形文化財に指定された後、[[川島町立川島中学校|川島中学校]]校門前に移築された。石棺の大きさは、外法長さ2.32メートル・幅0.8メートル、内法長さ1.93メートル・幅0.52メートルである。
[[ファイル:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|石棺（2013年9月）]] 副葬品は、江戸時代古墳上の社を立て直す最石棺内から鉄剣、[[大刀]]、[[鉄鏃]]、銅製の器が見つかったと伝えられているが、領主の秋元氏に渡されたが現在それらの遺物は所在不明である。墳丘から出土した[[埴輪]]から、[[6世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』pp365-366、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm 川島町｜石棺] - 川島町
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{japanese-history-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おおつかこふん}} [[Category:埼玉県の古墳]] [[Category:川島町の歴史]]

[[ファイル:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|大塚古墳（2013年9月）]] '''大塚古墳'''（おおつかこふん）は、[[埼玉県]][[川島町]]にある[[円墳]]である。東大塚古墳群を構成する1基。
直径36メートル・高さ5.5メートルの円墳と推定されるが、度重なる土取により墳丘は元の形がよくわからないほど大破している。江戸時代文化年間(1807 - 1817)に墳丘が削平された際に、[[緑泥石|緑泥]]片岩製の箱式[[石棺]]が掘り出された。この石棺は発見後長らく墳頂に露出放置されていたが、[[1961年]](昭和36年)1月25日付けで町指定有形文化財に指定された後、[[川島町立川島中学校|川島中学校]]校門前に移築された。石棺の大きさは、外法長さ2.32メートル・幅0.8メートル、内法長さ1.93メートル・幅0.52メートルである。また川島町生涯学習課は定期的に石棺の建て直し作業を実施している。
[[ファイル:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|石棺（2013年9月）]] 江戸時代に古墳上の社を立て直されており、その最、石棺内から鉄剣・[[大刀]]・[[鉄鏃]]・銅製の器が見つかったと伝えられている。これらは領主の秋元氏に渡されたとされるが、現在それらの遺物は所在不明である。墳丘から出土した[[埴輪]]から、[[6世紀]]中頃の築造と推定されている。
== 参考文献 == * 塩野博 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』pp365-366、さきたま出版会、2004年
== 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[埼玉県の古墳一覧]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (大塚古墳) refers to various {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} in Japan. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Sukagawa)|ja|大塚古墳 (須賀川市)}} * {{ill|Komagata Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|駒形大塚古墳}} - Located in {{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|ja|那須郡那珂川町}}, Tochigi Prefecture, part of the {{ill|Nasu Ogawa Kofun Cluster|ja|那須小川古墳群}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} - Located in {{il..."

revision.text:
* unomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yada Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the **Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group** (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a **diameter of 36 meters** and a **height of 5.5 meters**, but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the **Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period**, the mound was **partially leveled**, and a **{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist** was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained **exposed on top of the mound** for many years. On **January 25, 1961**, it was designated as a **town-designated tangible cultural property** (町指定有形文化財) and was later **relocated** to the front gate of **{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}**.
The coffin measures: * **Exterior dimensions:** **Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m** * **Interior dimensions:** **Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m**
The **Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division** conducts periodic **maintenance work** on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a **shrine on top of the mound** was rebuilt, **iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels** were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local **lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}**, but their **current whereabouts are unknown**.
Based on **{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)** found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been **constructed around the mid-6th century**.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the **Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group** (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a **diameter of 36 meters** and a **height of 5.5 meters**, but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the **Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period**, the mound was **partially leveled**, and a **{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist** was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained **exposed on top of the mound** for many years. On **January 25, 1961**, it was designated as a **town-designated tangible cultural property** (町指定有形文化財) and was later **relocated** to the front gate of **{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}**.
The coffin measures: * **Exterior dimensions:** **Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m** * **Interior dimensions:** **Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m**
The **Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division** conducts periodic **maintenance work** on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a **shrine on top of the mound** was rebuilt, **iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels** were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local **lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}**, but their **current whereabouts are unknown**.
Based on **{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)** found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been **constructed around the mid-6th century**.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the **Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group** (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a **diameter of 36 meters** and a **height of 5.5 meters**, but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the **Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period**, the mound was **partially leveled**, and a **{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist** was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained **exposed on top of the mound** for many years. On **January 25, 1961**, it was designated as a **town-designated tangible cultural property** (町指定有形文化財) and was later **relocated** to the front gate of **{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}**.
The coffin measures: * **Exterior dimensions:** **Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m** * **Interior dimensions:** **Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m**
The **Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division** conducts periodic **maintenance work** on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a **shrine on top of the mound** was rebuilt, **iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels** were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local **lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}**, but their **current whereabouts are unknown**.
Based on **{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)** found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been **constructed around the mid-6th century**.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the **Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group** (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a **diameter of 36 meters** and a **height of 5.5 meters**, but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the **Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period**, the mound was **partially leveled**, and a **{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist** was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained **exposed on top of the mound** for many years. On **January 25, 1961**, it was designated as a **town-designated tangible cultural property** (町指定有形文化財) and was later **relocated** to the front gate of **{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}**.
The coffin measures: * **Exterior dimensions:** **Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m** * **Interior dimensions:** **Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m**
The **Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division** conducts periodic **maintenance work** on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a **shrine on top of the mound** was rebuilt, **iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels** were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local **lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}**, but their **current whereabouts are unknown**.
Based on **{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)** found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been **constructed around the mid-6th century**.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the **Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group** (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a **diameter of 36 meters** and a **height of 5.5 meters**, but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the **Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period**, the mound was **partially leveled**, and a **{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist** was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained **exposed on top of the mound** for many years. On **January 25, 1961**, it was designated as a **town-designated tangible cultural property** (町指定有形文化財) and was later **relocated** to the front gate of **{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}**.
The coffin measures: * **Exterior dimensions:** **Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m** * **Interior dimensions:** **Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m**
The **Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division** conducts periodic **maintenance work** on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a **shrine on top of the mound** was rebuilt, **iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels** were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local **lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}**, but their **current whereabouts are unknown**.
Based on **{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)** found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been **constructed around the mid-6th century**.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ({{ill|Tachi (sword)|ja|大刀}}), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a {{ill|円墳|ja|Enpun}}, or circular burial mound, located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), iron arrowheads ({{ill|鉄鏃|ja}}), and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}

* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular burial mound]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Saitama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kawajima, Saitama]]
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en}}[[ja:川島町]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en}}[[ja:さいたま市]], Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|ヤマトタケル|de|Yamatotakeru|fr|Yamato Takeru|ko|야마토타케루|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:東田大塚古墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|ja|川島町}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
