revision.comment: Text replacement - "ox-shaped stone coffin" to "ox-type stone coffin"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).

After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''

These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Wikidata entry [[d:" to "Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|qq=|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type stone coffin|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the stone coffin remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The stone coffin (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|qq=|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|qq=|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17222628}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17222628}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.

Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. [[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] [[ja:川島町]] [[ja:方墳大塚古墳]] [[ja:さいたま市]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[ja:世田谷区]] [[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] [[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]] [[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] [[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]] [[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] [[ja:大垣市]] [[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] [[ja:本巣市]] [[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] [[ja:名古屋市守山区]] [[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊田市]] [[ja:須恵器]] [[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] [[ja:向日市]] [[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] [[ja:豊中市]] [[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] [[ja:羽曳野市]] [[ja:宮内庁]] [[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]] [[ja:東田大塚古墳]] [[ja:桜井市]] [[ja:纒向古墳群]] [[ja:中山大塚古墳]] [[ja:天理市]] [[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] [[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] [[ja:磯城郡田原本町]] [[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] [[ja:岡山市]] [[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] [[ja:倉敷市]] [[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] [[ja:福岡市西区]] [[ja:今宿古墳群]] [[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] [[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]] [[ja:田主丸古墳群]] [[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]] [[ja:大塚古墳]]
[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).

During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.

The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. [[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17222628}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages with conflicting qids]] to [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
