After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.

The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

[[ja:Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in [[:en:Kawajima, Saitama|Kawajima, Saitama]], Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ([[:en:大刀|Tachi]]), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto]]. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).

During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

{{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

{{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17222628]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Kawajima Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=List of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motosu|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota|qq=}}, Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Q11478280|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi|1=大刀}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. Designated a national historic site.

{{wikidata link|Q17222628}} [[Category:Kofun of Saitama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kawajima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama|1=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Q11478280|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi|1=大刀}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=list of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}

Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama|1=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Q11478280|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi|1=大刀}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大塚古墳 (川島町)]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.998306|N|139.492556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} [[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ōtsuka Kofun (2013)]] '''Ōtsuka Kofun''' (おおつかこふん) is a [[Round Kofun|circular Kofun]], located in {{ill|Kawajima, Saitama|en|Kawajima, Saitama|WD=Q1203970|lt=Kawajima, Saitama|1=Kawajima, Saitama}}, Japan. It is part of the '''Higashi-Ōtsuka Kofun Group''' (東大塚古墳群).
The mound is estimated to have originally had a '''diameter of 36 meters''' and a '''height of 5.5 meters''', but due to repeated soil excavation, its original form has been significantly damaged. During the '''Bunka era (1807–1817) of the Edo period''', the mound was '''partially leveled''', and a '''{{ill|Box-type Sarcophagus|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-type Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-type Sarcophagus|qq=}} made of chlorite schist''' was discovered.
After its discovery, the Sarcophagus remained '''exposed on top of the mound''' for many years. On '''January 25, 1961''', it was designated as a '''town-designated tangible cultural property''' (町指定有形文化財) and was later '''relocated''' to the front gate of '''{{ill|Kawajima Junior High School|ja|川島町立川島中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima Junior High School|1=Q11478280|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11478280}}'''.
The coffin measures: * '''Exterior dimensions:''' '''Length 2.32 m × Width 0.8 m''' * '''Interior dimensions:''' '''Length 1.93 m × Width 0.52 m'''
The '''Kawajima Town Lifelong Learning Division''' conducts periodic '''maintenance work''' on the coffin.
[[File:Kawajima Otuka Kohun Sekkan 1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Sarcophagus (2013)]] During the Edo period, when a '''shrine on top of the mound''' was rebuilt, '''iron swords ({{ill|鉄剣|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/鉄剣|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), long swords ({{ill|大刀|en|大刀|WD=Q1224104|lt=Tachi|1=大刀}}), {{ill|iron arrowheads|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iron arrowheads|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and bronze vessels''' were reportedly discovered inside the coffin. These artifacts were said to have been handed over to the local '''lord, the {{ill|Akimoto clan|ja|秋元氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akimoto clan|1=Akimoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594852}}''', but their '''current whereabouts are unknown'''.
Based on '''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figurines)''' found at the site, the mound is estimated to have been '''constructed around the mid-6th century'''.
== References == * Shiono, Hiroshi (2004). 『埼玉の古墳 比企・秩父』 ("Kofun of Saitama: Hiki & Chichibu"). Sakitama Publishing, pp. 365–366.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|ja|埼玉県の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Saitama Prefecture|1=list of Kofun in Saitama Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11426533}}
== External Links == * [https://www.town.kawajima.saitama.jp/1381.htm Kawajima Town Official Site - The Stone Coffin] (Japanese)
{{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|53.9|N|139|29|33.2|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Japanese-history-stub}}
* {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Kawajima)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kawajima|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawajima|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Saitama City|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama City|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōta, Tokyo|qq=}}, Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|qq=}}, Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hiruiōzuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruiōzuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgaki|qq=}}, Gifu Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sue ware|qq=}} designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mukō|qq=}}, Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Sakurazuka Tomb Group (Toyonaka City)|lt=Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyonaka|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}}. * {{ill|Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higaida Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makimuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenri|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawaramoto|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|qq=}}, Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imajuku Kofun Cluster|qq=}}.
